awareness.title: Blood Donation Awareness
awareness.subtitle: Know the facts, donate with confidence
eligibility_items: At least 90 days since your last donation

myths.fact: A healthy body replaces the donated volume within hours. A short rest is all that's needed.
Only a brief pinch from the needle — the whole process is safe and nearly painless.

myths.myth: You can only donate once a year
myths.fact: Healthy donors can give blood every 90 days — up to 3-4 times a year.
A new, sterile needle is used for every donation — there's no risk.

myths.myth: You can't donate with diabetes or high blood pressure
myths.fact: If it's under control and your doctor allows it, you can still donate.

after_items: Drink plenty of water and fluids
Avoid heavy work or exercise for a few hours
Eat nutritious food — rice, eggs, vegetables, fruit
Keep the bandage on for a few hours

footer.made_for: Made for the people of Savar, not for profit

common.setup_needed_desc: This page needs Supabase set up to work. See README.md.
